local_sources: Pseudonymous case intake, local evidence, DIRE or SARI screenshots and official public guidance

codex_context.purpose: Frame the case and identify unresolved professional classifications
codex_context.content: Pseudonymous case facts, validated evidence anchors, dates, competent chamber and unresolved decisions
codex_context.minimum_necessary: Use an opaque client reference and neutral case facts; exclude names, tax codes, contact data and addresses from public research and review payloads

codex_context.purpose: Relate selected official guidance to the proposed practice
codex_context.content: Generic official-source passages, territorial metadata and necessary pseudonymous case facts
codex_context.minimum_necessary: Keep public searches generic and include only case facts needed to assess source applicability

residual_risks.risk: Even pseudonymous activity, chamber and effective-date facts can identify a case when combined
residual_risks.mitigation: Keep identifiers local, use opaque case references, generic public searches and minimum case facts in Codex review

commercialista_notice.message_it: Le evidenze del caso restano nella cartella locale e vengono rappresentate con un riferimento pseudonimo. Codex esamina solo i fatti necessari e le fonti ufficiali pertinenti; ricerche SARI e payload non devono contenere identificativi del cliente.
commercialista_notice.message_en: Case evidence remains in the local folder and is represented by a pseudonymous reference. Codex reviews only necessary facts and relevant official sources; SARI searches and review payloads must not contain client identifiers.

limitations: Pseudonymized case facts may remain indirectly identifying in a narrow professional context
This is a design-time data-flow review, not a GDPR legal determination
